summary: Stellar audience tape of the legendary 6‑24‑70 Capitol Theatre show – crystal‑clear crowd ambience captures a transcendent performance highlighted by an epic Dark Star sandwich.
review: This recording, sourced from the audience, conveys the electric intensity and communal vibe of the June 24, 1970 Port Chester concert. The set is anchored by a monumental Dark Star → Attics → Dark Star → Sugar Magnolia sequence, blistering Not Fade Away‑Easy Wind, and a soaring St. Stephen > China Cat > I Know You Rider medley. While the tape has minor glitches and occasional disc‑ordering confusion, the sound is remarkably clear for an AUD and lets listeners feel the band‑and‑crowd interaction.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 80 % of reviews are 5‑star, praising both performance and recording; only a handful note minor flaws. The weighted emphasis on show quality (70 %) pushes the rating into the high‑4 range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Exceptionally clear for an audience tape of 1970; captures crowd reactions, applause and occasional vocal shouts. Minor issues: occasional drop‑outs, a few missing tracks, and confusing disc organization.

standout_songs: St. Stephen > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The show launches with a thunderous Not Fade Away that morphs into an inspired Easy Wind, then dives into a three‑part Dark Star sandwich (Dark Star → Attics → Dark Star → Sugar Magnolia → Dark Star). The energy stays relentless through St. Stephen > China Cat > I Know You Rider and rolls into a joyous acoustic/NRPS intermission before closing with Swing Low, Sweet Chariot.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his soaring lead work, ‘smoking’ tone, and the way he drives the Dark Star improvisations.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for sustaining beautiful harmonic notes during Dark Star and adding depth to the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned for rhythmic guitar work that weaves tightly with Garcia, especially in the China Cat > Rider transition.
band_member_comments.Keys: Few explicit comments; some reviewers simply note the overall mix without specific keyboard focus.
band_member_comments.Drums: Praised as a ‘percussion machine’; Bill and Mickey’s tight, driving beats are highlighted throughout the set.
